headline: Fi's Starlink-enabled pet tracker found my dog when LTE couldn't
summary: Fi Ultra is the first Starlink-enabled pet tracker, expanding beyond GPS and LTE. It offers automatic failover to T-Mobile's T-Satellite service in dead zones. Owners can track pets anywhere in the US using SpaceX's Starlink satellites.

headline: The Steam Machine fits my TV, my desk, and my life
summary: The author spent weeks playing games on Valve's Steam Machine. Despite owning a PS5 and Xbox Series X, the Steam Machine still holds unique appeal. The device received a 6 in a previous review, a sentiment the author doesn't disagree with.

headline: The robotaxi law that could ban Tesla
summary: A long-standing debate questions if cameras alone are sufficient for safe autonomous driving. Tesla has bet billions on AI and cameras for its self-driving technology. A new law may mandate additional sensors like lidar and radar, potentially impacting Tesla's approach.

headline: Samsung will launch its new wide foldable on July 22nd
summary: Samsung announced its next Galaxy Unpacked event for July 22nd. The event's tagline, "A new shape unfolds," hints at a new device. Rumors suggest a new shorter, wider foldable phone format is expected.

headline: Nintendo Switch 2 Leak Reveals The Crew Motorfest, With Physical Launch Via Game-Key Card
summary: A leak suggests Ubisoft will soon announce The Crew Motorfest for Nintendo Switch 2. The game is expected to receive a physical release. Physical copies will be available via Game-Key Cards.

headline: 'It Never Trickles Down' — Bernie Sanders Hits Out at Microsoft Over Xbox Layoffs and Console Price Rises, Says They Prove Corporate Tax Breaks Do Not Create Jobs
summary: Sen. Bernie Sanders criticized Microsoft over recent Xbox layoffs. He also highlighted the company's console price increases. Sanders argues these actions demonstrate that corporate tax breaks fail to create jobs.

headline: Subnautica 2 Early Access 1.1 Update Improves Creature Flinches So You'll Know When You've Hit Them With the Survival Multitool — but No, You Still Can't Kill Fish
summary: Subnautica 2's Early Access 1.1 update releases today, July 8th. The update improves creature flinch animations for better combat feedback. Players still cannot kill fish despite the improvements.

headline: 'They Now Wait for This Storm to Pass' — PS5 Owners Are Cancelling PlayStation Plus Subscriptions to Protest Sony Killing Discs, but Analysts Say It Won't Make Any Difference
summary: PS5 owners are canceling PlayStation Plus subscriptions in protest. The protest targets Sony's decision to stop releasing games on physical discs. Analysts believe this gesture will not alter Sony's strategy.

headline: Ruth Ellis, last woman to be hanged in UK, gets conditional posthumous pardon
summary: Ruth Ellis, the last woman executed in the UK, has received a conditional posthumous pardon. She was hanged in 1955 after being convicted of murdering her lover. This decision marks a significant development in her historic case.

headline: Chris Mason: It's far from certain whether by-election will strengthen Farage
summary: Chris Mason analyzes the uncertain impact of an upcoming by-election on Nigel Farage. The Reform UK leader faces a challenge from candidates like Count Binface. Major parties have decided not to field candidates in Clacton.

headline: Police name man wanted over murders of wife and daughters
summary: Police have named Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma as wanted for the murders of his wife and daughters. An international manhunt is underway for Tshuma. He is known to have left the country via Heathrow Airport.

headline: Amber heat health alerts in effect as UK set for one of longest-lasting heatwaves since 1976
summary: Amber heat health alerts are currently in effect across the UK. Temperatures could soar to 36C (97F) in parts of southern England this week. The UK is bracing for one of its longest heatwaves since 1976.
